question: What are the main functions of the pressurizer?

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer, integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and separated by a baffle plate, regulates Reactor Coolant System (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing real-time pressure and level data to control systems.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section describes the testing and inspection requirements for the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) components in the NuScale reactor, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for Class 2 components. It details how DHRS actuation valves are tested during cold shutdown conditions, outlines the procedures for valve exercising and position verification, and specifies that a one-time in-situ test of the DHRS function will be performed on the first installed reactor module as part of the initial test program.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure-retaining component and barrier to fission product release, supporting various reactor systems and components. Materials for the RPV are selected according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant's design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains proper coolant chemistry and boron concentration, and is engineered to prevent fuel design limit violations during all operating conditions.

edges.description: Section A identifies the pressurizer as a component within the reactor vessel, while Section B provides specific details about the pressurizer's location and its functional integration with the reactor vessel, connecting them through the physical and operational relationship within the reactor coolant system.

Section A identifies the pressurizer as a component of the reactor coolant system, while Section B provides a detailed description of the pressurizer's design and its principal function within that system.

Section A identifies the pressurizer as a component within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B specifies that the pressurizer is an integral part of the reactor coolant system, thus Section B provides a broader system context for the component described in Section A.
